translation: Therefore I will allot him a portion with the many, and with the mighty he shall divide the spoil—because he poured out his life unto death and was counted among the transgressors; and he bore the sin of many, and for the transgressors he will intercede.

alternate_translations.translation: So I will give him a share among the great, and he will divide the plunder with the strong—since he poured out his soul to death and was numbered with the sinners; and he carried the sin of many, and he will plead for the offenders.
alternate_translations.notes: “Intercede/plead” reflects the sense of יפגיע (often rendered “intercede,” “plead,” or “make supplication” in this verse). “Spoil/plunder” corresponds to שלל, “share” to חלק, and “sinners/offenders” to פשעים depending on interpretive emphasis.

notes: This is a Hebrew verse (commonly Isaiah 53:12) describing vindication after suffering. Key terms: “poured out his life/soul to death” (ערה למות נפשו) and “counted/numbered among the transgressors” (נמנה). פשעים (“transgressors/offenders/sinners”) appears again in the final clause, where יפגיע is traditionally taken as “intercede/plead” on their behalf. The line “he bore the sin of many” (חטא-רבים נשא) conveys substitutionary/representative bearing of guilt.
